_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Applied social sciences involves using an understanding of the social sciences to evaluate and challenge assumptions made in relevant projects.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing and executing applied research projects with clear organizational relevance, balancing research rigor with practical applicability.
comments: DDaT skill intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-007 (AB, cosine: 0.338), AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.337).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.1/10 (raw 0.509). Shared vocabulary: [applied, projects]. DDaT-only terms [assumptions, challenge, evaluate, involves, made, relevant] vs WIDAI-only terms [applicability, balancing, clear, designing, executing, organizational] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.871, R=0.866). Balanced P/R — the 20-word DDaT element and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:43.5%, N:56.4%, C:0.1%). No dominant classification for "Applied social sciences involves using an understanding of the social sciences t" vs "Skill in designing and executing applied research projects with clear organizati" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.452 < STS 0.509 (gap -0.058). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [assumptions, challenge, evaluate] and [applicability, balancing, clear] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.3%. Shared tokens: [applied, projects]. DDaT-only: [assumptions, challenge, evaluate, involves, made, relevant]. WIDAI-only: [applicability, balancing, clear, designing, executing, organizational].
